# uritools

This module provides RFC 3986 compliant functions for parsing,
classifying and composing URIs and URI references, largely replacing
the Python Standard Library's `urllib.parse` module.

```pycon
>>> from uritools import uricompose, urijoin, urisplit, uriunsplit
>>> uricompose(scheme='foo', host='example.com', port=8042,
...            path='/over/there', query={'name': 'ferret'},
...            fragment='nose')
'foo://example.com:8042/over/there?name=ferret#nose'
>>> parts = urisplit(_)
>>> parts.scheme
'foo'
>>> parts.authority
'example.com:8042'
>>> parts.getport(default=80)
8042
>>> parts.getquerydict().get('name')
['ferret']
>>> parts.isuri()
True
>>> parts.isabsuri()
False
>>> urijoin(uriunsplit(parts), '/right/here?name=swallow#beak')
'foo://example.com:8042/right/here?name=swallow#beak'
```

For various reasons, `urllib.parse` and its Python 2 predecessor
`urlparse` are not compliant with current Internet standards.  As
stated in [Lib/urllib/parse.py](https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3.8/Lib/urllib/parse.py):

> RFC 3986 is considered the current standard and any future changes
> to urlparse module should conform with it.  The urlparse module is
> currently not entirely compliant with this RFC due to defacto
> scenarios for parsing, and for backward compatibility purposes,
> some parsing quirks from older RFCs are retained.

This module aims to provide fully RFC 3986 compliant replacements for
the most commonly used functions found in `urllib.parse`.  It also
includes functions for distinguishing between the different forms of
URIs and URI references, and for conveniently creating URIs from their
individual components.

## Installation

uritools is available from [PyPI] and can be installed by running:

```
pip install uritools
```

modules.name: uritools
documentation:
RFC 3986 compliant, scheme-agnostic replacement for `urllib.parse`.

This module defines RFC 3986 compliant replacements for the most
commonly used functions of the Python Standard Library
{mod}`urllib.parse` module.

functions.name: uritools.uriencode
documentation: Encode a URI string or string component.

functions.name: uritools.uridecode
documentation: Decode a URI string or string component.

functions.name: uritools.uridefrag
documentation: Remove an existing fragment component from a URI reference string.

functions.name: uritools.urisplit
documentation:
Split a well-formed URI reference string into a tuple with five
components corresponding to a URI's general structure:

```
<scheme>://<authority>/<path>?<query>#<fragment>
```

functions.name: uritools.uriunsplit
documentation:
Combine the elements of a five-item iterable into a URI reference's
string representation.

functions.name: uritools.urijoin
documentation:
Convert a URI reference relative to a base URI to its target URI
string.

functions.name: uritools.isuri
documentation: Return {const}`True` if `uristring` is a URI.

functions.name: uritools.isabsuri
documentation: Return {const}`True` if `uristring` is an absolute URI.

functions.name: uritools.isnetpath
documentation: Return {const}`True` if `uristring` is a network-path reference.

functions.name: uritools.isabspath
documentation: Return {const}`True` if `uristring` is an absolute-path reference.

functions.name: uritools.isrelpath
documentation: Return {const}`True` if `uristring` is a relative-path reference.

functions.name: uritools.issamedoc
documentation: Return {const}`True` if `uristring` is a same-document reference.

functions.name: uritools.uricompose
documentation: Compose a URI reference string from its individual components.

classes.name: uritools.DefragResult

methods.name: uritools.DefragResult.geturi
documentation: Return the recombined version of the original URI as a string.

methods.name: uritools.DefragResult.getfragment
documentation:
Return the decoded fragment identifier, or `default` if the
original URI did not contain a fragment component.

class_variables.name: uritools.DefragResult.__slots__
documentation: prevent creation of instance dictionary
Class to hold {func}`uridefrag` results.

classes.name: uritools.SplitResult

methods.name: uritools.SplitResult.geturi
documentation:
Return the re-combined version of the original URI reference as a
string.

methods.name: uritools.SplitResult.getscheme
documentation:
Return the URI scheme in canonical (lowercase) form, or `default`
if the original URI reference did not contain a scheme component.

methods.name: uritools.SplitResult.getauthority
documentation:
Return the decoded userinfo, host and port subcomponents of the URI
authority as a three-item tuple.

methods.name: uritools.SplitResult.getuserinfo
documentation:
Return the decoded userinfo subcomponent of the URI authority, or
`default` if the original URI reference did not contain a
userinfo field.

methods.name: uritools.SplitResult.gethost
documentation:
Return the decoded host subcomponent of the URI authority as a
string or an {mod}`ipaddress` address object, or `default` if
the original URI reference did not contain a host.

methods.name: uritools.SplitResult.getport
documentation:
Return the port subcomponent of the URI authority as an
{class}`int`, or `default` if the original URI reference did
not contain a port or if the port was empty.

methods.name: uritools.SplitResult.getpath
documentation: Return the normalized decoded URI path.

methods.name: uritools.SplitResult.getquery
documentation:
Return the decoded query string, or `default` if the original URI
reference did not contain a query component.

methods.name: uritools.SplitResult.getquerydict
documentation:
Split the query component into individual `name=value` pairs
separated by `sep` and return a dictionary of query variables.
The dictionary keys are the unique query variable names and
the values are lists of values for each name.

methods.name: uritools.SplitResult.getquerylist
documentation:
Split the query component into individual `name=value` pairs
separated by `sep`, and return a list of `(name, value)`
tuples.

methods.name: uritools.SplitResult.getfragment
documentation:
Return the decoded fragment identifier, or `default` if the
original URI reference did not contain a fragment component.

methods.name: uritools.SplitResult.isuri
documentation: Return {const}`True` if this is a URI.

methods.name: uritools.SplitResult.isabsuri
documentation: Return {const}`True` if this is an absolute URI.

methods.name: uritools.SplitResult.isnetpath
documentation: Return {const}`True` if this is a network-path reference.

methods.name: uritools.SplitResult.isabspath
documentation: Return {const}`True` if this is an absolute-path reference.

methods.name: uritools.SplitResult.isrelpath
documentation: Return {const}`True` if this is a relative-path reference.

methods.name: uritools.SplitResult.issamedoc
documentation: Return {const}`True` if this is a same-document reference.

methods.name: uritools.SplitResult.transform
documentation:
Transform a URI reference relative to `self` into a
{class}`SplitResult` representing its target URI.

class_variables.name: uritools.SplitResult.__slots__
documentation: prevent creation of instance dictionary
Base class to hold {func}`urisplit` results.

class_variables.name: uritools.SplitResultBytes.__slots__
documentation: prevent creation of instance dictionary

class_variables.name: uritools.SplitResultString.__slots__
documentation: prevent creation of instance dictionary
